title: Broker Registration for Medicines
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: A practical UK guide to medicines broker registration: who must register with the MHRA, GDP brokering obligations, quality systems and inspection readiness.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury pharmaceutical banner in a cool teal and cyan palette layered over deep navy, with a single subtle green accent. The scene shows an out-of-focus modern pharmaceutical logistics and trade environment: blurred rows of sealed, unbranded medicine cartons on bright shelving behind a sharply focused gloved hand reviewing a clean transaction document on a tablet, suggesting brokered supply with no physical handling of stock. Soft natural side light falls across brushed-steel and glass surfaces, with a very shallow depth of field producing creamy bokeh highlights in teal and cyan. Calm, premium, precise and trustworthy mood, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos, no readable labels.

faqs.q: Do I need a WDA(H) or a broker registration?
faqs.a: It depends entirely on whether you take ownership or possession of the medicines. If you only negotiate buying and selling between authorised parties and never hold, store or supply stock yourself, you need a broker registration. The moment you own the goods or control their storage or transport, you require a Wholesale Dealer's Authorisation, WDA(H), instead.

faqs.q: Does a medicines broker need a Responsible Person?
faqs.a: A broker is not legally required to name a Responsible Person in the same way a WDA(H) holder must, because no physical operations take place. However, the GDP brokering chapter still requires a documented quality system with clear, competent accountability for compliance. In practice the MHRA expects to see a named, suitably experienced individual owning that responsibility.

faqs.q: What records must a medicines broker keep?
faqs.a: Brokers must retain a record of every transaction they arrange, capturing the product, quantity, batch where available, the supplier, the recipient and the relevant dates. These records must follow ALCOA+ data integrity principles and be readily retrievable for MHRA inspection. Evidence of counterparty qualification, such as dated checks of supplier and customer authorisation status, should be kept alongside them.
